title: Tradition Arc
description: SNG-203 tier 2. A three-beat authored path per tradition: find the teacher (The Finding) -> prove the tradition's values under pressure (The Proving) -> learn the capstone as a SCENE (The Ultimate). One per traditionId. Gates on DEMONSTRATED DISPOSITION, not level — you reach the ashwarden teacher by acting like one along the death axis. Ties into the teacher-gate already built (SNG-100b: tier IV-V abilities gate on teachers[trad]={met,willing}) and the capstone band (SNG-130). Each beat's `quest` is a full quest_structure-shaped object OR a ref to one; the arc is the ordered spine that binds the three. GM generates the other 23 via generate('tradition_arc', ctx) against this schema, in-grain for the tradition's axis/disposition/craft.

teacher.description: the tradition's master. A legend-tier or named NPC; may be authored or minted.

disposition.description: what the teacher embodies — the tradition's values made a person

capstoneAbility.description: the ability id the tradition's Ultimate beat makes learnable. MUST reference an existing ability (SNG-203 §4 guard: verify capstone coverage before authoring the Proving beat). For ashwarden: 'the_cut_thread'.

beats.description: exactly three, ordered: finding -> proving -> ultimate.

gate.description: what UNLOCKS this beat. Finding: demonstrate the tradition's disposition (act on its axis), never a level bar. Proving: the teacher found + willing. Ultimate: the proving passed.

quest.description: a full quest_structure-shaped object for this beat, OR a string ref to a quest id. The finding/proving carry real stages+routes+outcomes; the ultimate's outcome is the capstone-learning SCENE.

ultimateIsAScene.description: learning the capstone is a narrated moment (SNG-197 moment-doctrine), never a menu unlock. The teacher gives it; the giving is played.

designLaws: Find, then prove, then receive. You do not buy a tradition's ultimate skill; you become the kind of person it is given to.

The Proving IS the tradition's values under pressure — for a HARM people it costs blood, for a KNOW people it costs a comfortable ignorance, for ashwarden it is an ending attended rather than fought. The proving must be the tradition, not a generic trial.
The gate is disposition, not level. A low-level character who acts like an ashwarden reaches the teacher before a high-level one who does not.
The capstone must already exist (SNG-130 / verify). Never promise a skill the game cannot grant.
The Ultimate is a scene. The moment doctrine (SNG-197) applies: the player should feel they were GIVEN something, by someone, at a cost.
